transformation_type: aldehyde one-carbon homologation to terminal alkyne
mechanism_type: dibromoolefination followed by strong-base dehydrohalogenation and acetylide hydrolysis
common_conditions: form dibromoolefin from aldehyde with carbon tetrabromide and phosphine, then treat with n-BuLi and hydrolyze
description_short: One-carbon homologation of aldehydes to terminal alkynes through dibromoolefin intermediates and organolithium-mediated elimination.

transformation_type: primary/secondary alcohol oxidation to aldehyde or ketone
mechanism_type: NCS/DMS activation to chlorodimethylsulfonium species followed by alkoxysulfonium elimination
common_conditions: generate Corey-Kim reagent from NCS and dimethyl sulfide at low temperature, then add alcohol and Et3N
description_short: Mild oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones using the NCS/DMS/Et3N reagent system via alkoxysulfonium intermediates.

transformation_type: hydroxy acid cyclization to medium- or large-ring lactone
mechanism_type: 2-pyridinethiol ester activation followed by intramolecular lactonization under high dilution
common_conditions: activate hydroxy acid with dipyridyl disulfide and phosphine, then heat under high dilution for cyclization
description_short: Macrolactonization method that activates hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters before intramolecular closure to medium- and large-ring lactones.

mechanism_type: thionocarbonate formation followed by trialkyl phosphite-promoted syn elimination
common_conditions: convert vicinal diols to cyclic thionocarbonates and heat with phosphite to expel sulfur/phosphorus byproducts and form alkene
description_short: Stereospecific deoxygenation of vicinal diols to alkenes through cyclic thionocarbonate intermediates and phosphite-triggered elimination.

mechanism_type: thermal electrocyclic ring opening to dicarbonylnitrile ylide followed by cyclization to rearranged oxazole
common_conditions: thermal rearrangement of oxazole carbonyl isomers, sometimes coupled to thionation or base-induced ring editing
description_short: Thermal rearrangement of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products and related heterocycle-edited scaffolds.
